core_rule: Automatically discover and apply every safe accessible non-secret value and every already-configured approved secret reference. Request human action only when a protected value or account action is genuinely inaccessible to the current authorised toolset.

AUTO_APPLY: public service URLs already committed as approved configuration
redirect URIs derived from verified domains
AUTO_USE_REFERENCE_ONLY: GitHub Actions secrets available to the workflow
environment secrets available to an approved deployment job
provider-vault secrets available through an approved connector
HUMAN_ACTION_REQUIRED: OAuth consent where the provider requires the account owner
creation or rotation of a missing protected value when no authorised secret-management connector exists
NEVER_AUTO_DISCOVER_FROM_UNSAFE_LOCATION: uploaded files not explicitly designated as a secret source
password managers without an approved connector and explicit scope

execution_order: derive safe public and deterministic values
reuse existing approved secret references without exposing values
show one consolidated HUMAN ACTION REQUIRED card only for unresolved protected values or owner-only steps
resume automatically after readiness becomes green

requirements: exact provider page or repository settings path

proof_requirements: secret reference presence checked without disclosure
runtime state remains separate from repository configuration state
rollback: Revert the policy and setup-automation pull request. Remove generated non-secret configuration only after reference checks. Existing GitHub Actions or provider-vault secret values are never deleted by this policy.
